message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Task #4 ready for review, need 9/9">
Task #4 (HTMX empty state transition) committed and pushed (4e049aa). CI is running.

ALL REVIEWERS: Please review and send consent or concerns. We need 9/9 consensus.

Note: Carson pushed before receiving the process update — that's fine. The new flow (consensus before push) applies starting with Task #5.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Let me review commit 4e049aa for Task #4.

input.description: See files changed in Task #4 commit

content.content:
commit 4e049aa51c00ac6e0983793d1fd091ee7ab9191c
Good "git" signature for john@johnwilger.com with ED25519 key SHA256:Fa/i+mjuGYJd8KbzsXhV2I87PNZ8q9S/k8BCW2ayIr0
Author: John Wilger <john@johnwilger.com>
Date:   Sun Feb 8 14:37:58 2026 -0800

    Add HTMX empty state transition when last todo is deleted
    
    The empty state message ("No todos yet.") is now always present in the
    DOM with id="todo-empty", hidden via the hidden attribute when items
    exist. The todo-focus.js script toggles visibility after HTMX delete
    and add swaps, so the empty state appears when the last item is
    deleted and disappears when the first item is added.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 static/js/todo-focus.js | 53 +++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++----
 templates/todos.html    |  4 +---
 tests/api/todos.rs      | 21 ++++++++++++++++----
 3 files changed, 67 insertions(+), 11 deletions(-)

content.text: Good. I've now reviewed the full commit thoroughly. Let me send my review.

input.content:
**Lea Verou -- Task #4 Review: CONSENT** Reviewed commit 4e049aa. This is a clean, well-scoped change. Here's my assessment: **Template change (todos.html line 39) -- excellent:** The empty state `

` is now always in the DOM with `id="todo-empty"` and uses the `hidden` attribute for visibility toggling. This is the correct pattern -- identical to what we already do for error elements (`#add-todo-error` with `role="alert"`). The `hidden` attribute is a native HTML boolean attribute that maps directly to `display: none` in the browser's UA stylesheet, and our `.todo-empty:empty { display: none }` won't interfere because the element always has text content. The server-side conditional `{% if !todos.is_empty() %} hidden{% endif %}` ensures correct initial render for both JS and no-JS cases. Progressive enhancement baseline maintained. **JavaScript (todo-focus.js) -- well-structured:** 1. **`updateEmptyState()` function is clean.** It queries the DOM for `.todo-item` children to determine emptiness, then toggles `hidden` on both the list and the message. Using `hidden` attribute (not CSS classes) is the right choice -- it's semantic, works without CSS, and is the platform's built-in visibility mechanism. 2. **Delete path:** The `pendingDeleteSwap` flag gates empty state updates to only fire after delete swaps, not after every `htmx:afterSettle`. Good scoping. 3. **Add path:** The `htmx:afterRequest` listener on the add form correctly unhides the list when the first item is added. The `e.detail.successful` check ensures failed submissions don't toggle state. Using `htmx:afterRequest` (rather than `htmx:afterSettle`) here is fine -- the add form's `hx-swap="beforeend"` will have already inserted the new `

  • ` by the time `afterRequest` fires, so `querySelector(".todo-item")` will find it. 4. **Bidirectional:** Both transitions are handled -- empty-to-populated (add) and populated-to-empty (delete). No state can get stuck. **CSS -- no changes needed:** The existing `.todo-empty` styles (color, font-size, text-align, padding) apply correctly. The `hidden` attribute in the browser UA stylesheet handles visibility. No CSS conflicts. **Tests -- good coverage:** The updated tests verify the always-in-DOM pattern: `id="todo-empty"` is present in both empty and populated states, with `hidden` attribute correctly toggled. This ensures server-side rendering is correct. **One observation (not blocking):** The `

    ` element doesn't have `role="status"` or `aria-live`. When the empty state message appears after the last delete, screen readers won't automatically announce "No todos yet." -- the user would need to navigate to discover it. This is arguably fine because the focus management from Task #3 already moves focus to the add input when the list empties, which is a sufficient cue. But Heydon may want to weigh in on whether an `aria-live="polite"` announcement via the existing `#live-region` would be better UX for screen reader users. Not a blocker from my side. **Verdict: I consent.** The always-in-DOM pattern with `hidden` attribute toggling is the correct web platform approach, the JS is minimal and well-scoped, and progressive enhancement is maintained.
input.summary: Task #4 review: CONSENT, empty state approach correct

content.text: Consent sent for Task #4. Standing by for Task #5 (HTMX inline editing) -- that one will follow the new consensus-before-push flow. I'm particularly interested in reviewing the inline edit pattern since I wrote the CSS for `.todo-item__edit-form` and its children in my earlier Driver session. I'll want to make sure Carson's HTMX approach works well with those existing styles.
